rationale.root_cause: They identified the unsafe strcpy on argv data but cited the wrong line (program.c:12 vs expected line 11) and did not name it a global_buffer_overflow. The criterion requires both the exact line and the specific sanitizer class name.
rationale.local_fix: They switched to strncpy and ensured null-termination, but did not reject overlong input before copying as required.
rationale.global_fix: They advised adopting bounded string APIs (e.g., strlcpy/strncpy) and input validation across the codebase, which removes raw, unbounded string handling from the data path.
